strategy: He turns to Statue form and becomes immune to all damage at the end of Round 1, Round 4, and every fifth Round after (1, 4, 9, 14...), for 2 Rounds each time.
Attack him during the Statue phase to force him straight out of it, making him vulnerable again, so do not waste your big hit into the immune window.

If the Statue phase expires without you hitting him, he casts Medusa and turns a wizard to stone for 99 Rounds; you can still be healed while statued and can Pass through the 77-card Pass deck or play the single Break Free.
You can dispel the Statue polymorph with Vaporize to keep the pressure on.
He resists Ice 76% and other schools 46% but takes 30% more from Fire, so bring Fire; set up while he is stone-immune, then hit the Round he is exposed.
cheatNotes: At the end of Round 1, Round 4, and every fifth Round after, he casts Power Nova on himself, dealing no damage but turning to Statue form for 2 Rounds.
While in Statue form he is immune to all damage.
Attacking him during the Statue phase forces him out of it, making him vulnerable again.
If the Statue phase expires without you hitting him, he casts Medusa and turns a wizard to stone for 99 Rounds.
The polymorph deck is 77 Pass cards plus one Break Free (Myth); you can discard and Pass normally, and you can be healed while statued.
If every wizard is already a statue, he skips Medusa after the Statue phase ends naturally.
This Medusa cheat can be dispelled with Vaporize.
